decomposition_notes: Single-goal shape — all work is in one tightly code-coupled cluster. The requirements doc explicitly states that classifier changes and all call-site updates in app.py must happen atomically (Assumptions section), making a multi-goal split unsafe. Natural seams: (1) classifier function update in classifier.py, (2) colour mapping, SIGNAL_ORDER, and call sites in app.py, (3) test updates. Five tasks sequenced by dependency. Open questions (2-3 cond + RS 10-29 classification, depot summary table column grouping) are deferred as pitfalls, not tasks.

goals.title: Replace 5-tier classifier with 7-tier direction-aware classification
goals.why: The current 5-tier snapshot system cannot distinguish a stock beginning to deteriorate from one stuck weak for weeks, and offers no signal for stocks recovering from weakness. Users act too late. The 7-tier system surfaces Warning 1-2 scans earlier and Recovered before re-qualification for Buy, while tightening the Buy RS floor from 70 to 85 to reduce false Buy signals.
goals.what: Update classify_signal() to implement the 7-tier evaluation order, raise _RS_BUY_MIN from 70.0 to 85.0, update SIGNAL_ORDER and colour mapping in app.py, update all tier-label string-match call sites in app.py atomically, and update tests. screener.py and indicators.py are not modified.
goals.where_context: classifier.py (classify_signal function), app.py (colour mapping, SIGNAL_ORDER, Buy-filter call sites, sound-alert call sites), tests/ directory
goals.acceptance_criteria:
classify_signal() implements the 7-step evaluation order exactly as specified
_RS_BUY_MIN is 85.0
SIGNAL_ORDER is [Buy, Bullish, Recovered, Neutral, Warning, Chronic, Sell-Panic]
Colour mapping covers all 7 tiers with the colours from the Sketch table
All string-match call sites in app.py that previously referenced Bearish are updated to Warning or Chronic
All existing tests remain green
New tests cover all 7 tier return paths and RS=85 boundary
pitfalls: screener.py and indicators.py must not be modified — classify_signal() must stay in or be moved to classifier.py
All tier-label string matches in app.py must be updated atomically — a partial update silently breaks colour mapping, Buy filters, and sound alerts without raising an exception
Stocks with 2-3 conditions and RS 10-29 are Warning (only RS < 10 triggers Sell-Panic) — do not introduce a separate RS floor for Warning
During a broad market correction many stocks may simultaneously drop to Warning — this is a known risk in Assumptions, not a bug to fix

tasks.title: Audit classify_signal() location and all tier-label call sites in app.py
tasks.description: Read-only audit: identify the exact file and line where classify_signal() is defined, confirm _RS_BUY_MIN location, and enumerate every place in app.py that string-matches tier labels (colour mapping dict keys, SIGNAL_ORDER list, Buy-filter conditionals, sound-alert conditionals). classifier.py already exists in the project and is the expected home — verify this. No code changes in this task.
tasks.why: The Constraints section forbids modifying screener.py and indicators.py. Without a complete audit of call sites, a partial update will silently break colour mapping or alerts with no exception raised.
tasks.what: Grep and read app.py, screener.py, classifier.py for: (1) definition of classify_signal() and _RS_BUY_MIN, (2) every string literal referencing Buy, Bullish, Bearish, Neutral, Sell-Panic in app.py, (3) the current SIGNAL_ORDER definition location.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Exact file:line of classify_signal() definition identified
Exact file:line of _RS_BUY_MIN identified
All string-match call sites for tier labels in app.py listed with file:line references
Current SIGNAL_ORDER definition location identified
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Use grep or file reads only — no code modifications in this task
pitfalls: Check for tier labels in f-strings and formatted strings, not only plain string literals and dict keys

key_files.note: Audit colour mapping, SIGNAL_ORDER, Buy filters, sound alerts
Confirm classify_signal() is NOT defined here

verification_steps.step_text: Confirm file and line number of classify_signal() definition is recorded
Confirm a complete list of all tier-label string literals in app.py is recorded, covering colour mapping, SIGNAL_ORDER, and all conditionals

manual_tests: Grep output confirms no tier-label string sites were missed
edge_cases: Check for tier labels in f-strings, not just plain string literals

tasks.title: Implement 7-tier evaluation order in classifier.py with RS Buy threshold 85
tasks.description: Update classifier.py: change _RS_BUY_MIN from 70.0 to 85.0, replace the 5-tier if/elif chain in classify_signal() with the 7-step evaluation order: (1) RS < 10 Sell-Panic, (2) cond == 8 and RS >= 85 Buy, (3) cond >= 6 and RS >= 50 Bullish, (4) cond >= 4 and RS >= 50 Recovered, (5) cond >= 4 Neutral, (6) cond >= 2 Warning, (7) else Chronic. Preserve the existing function signature and ValueError on RS=None. Do not modify screener.py or indicators.py.
tasks.why: The core classification logic is the foundation all other tasks depend on. The worked edge case (cond=6, RS=40 must return Neutral) confirms that RS gates the upper tiers and evaluation must fall through step 5.
tasks.what: Edit classifier.py: update _RS_BUY_MIN constant and replace the if/elif body of classify_signal(). Update SIGNAL_ORDER from 5 entries to 7 entries.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
classify_signal() returns Sell-Panic when RS < 10 regardless of cond
classify_signal() returns Buy only when cond == 8 and RS >= 85
classify_signal() returns Bullish when cond >= 6 and RS >= 50 (including cond=8 RS=50-84)
classify_signal() returns Recovered when cond >= 4 and RS >= 50 and cond < 6
classify_signal() returns Neutral when cond >= 4 and RS < 50 (including cond=6-7 RS<50)
classify_signal() returns Warning when cond >= 2 and RS >= 10
classify_signal() returns Chronic when cond <= 1 and RS >= 10
classify_signal() raises ValueError when RS is None
_RS_BUY_MIN is 85.0
SIGNAL_ORDER == [Buy, Bullish, Recovered, Neutral, Warning, Chronic, Sell-Panic]
screener.py and indicators.py are unmodified (verify with git diff)
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Preserve the existing function signature of classify_signal() exactly
Return tier label strings with exact casing: Buy, Bullish, Recovered, Neutral, Warning, Chronic, Sell-Panic
pitfalls: Step 5 (Neutral) catches cond >= 4 with RS < 50 — this includes cond=6-7 with RS < 50. Do not add an upper bound on cond for step 5.
The evaluation order matters: step 4 (Recovered) must come before step 5 (Neutral) so RS >= 50 stocks with cond 4-5 get Recovered, not Neutral.

verification_steps.step_text: Trace cond=6, RS=40 through new evaluation: step 3 fails (RS<50), step 4 fails (RS<50), step 5 fires (cond>=4) → Neutral
Trace cond=8, RS=84 through new evaluation: step 2 fails (RS<85), step 3 fires (cond>=6 RS>=50) → Bullish

verification_steps.expected_result: Empty diff — no changes to screener.py or indicators.py

edge_cases: cond=2 rs=9 → Sell-Panic (RS override fires before Warning check)
cond=5 rs=50 → Recovered (not Neutral, RS>=50 gates Recovered)

tasks.title: Update SIGNAL_ORDER, colour mapping, and all tier-label call sites in app.py
tasks.description: Using the call-site list from task 0, update app.py atomically: replace SIGNAL_ORDER with the 7-tier list (import from classifier.py), add colour entries for Recovered (teal/cyan), Warning (orange), Chronic (dark grey/white), remove or update the Bearish entry, update any Buy-filter conditionals and sound-alert conditionals that string-match old tier names. All changes in one commit.
tasks.why: The Assumptions section requires atomic update of all call sites. A partial update silently breaks colour mapping for stocks the classifier now labels Warning, Recovered, or Chronic — they render with no background colour, not an error.
tasks.what: In app.py: (1) Update SIGNAL_ORDER reference or import from classifier.py. (2) In _SIGNAL_COLOURS dict add Recovered (teal bg, dark text), Warning (orange bg, dark text), Chronic (dark grey bg, white text); remove Bearish key. (3) Update all conditionals and comparisons from old tier names to new ones. (4) Remove all remaining string literals referencing Bearish.
tasks.where_context: app.py — all locations from the task 0 audit
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
SIGNAL_ORDER in app.py or imported from classifier equals [Buy, Bullish, Recovered, Neutral, Warning, Chronic, Sell-Panic]
_SIGNAL_COLOURS has keys for all 7 tiers
No string literal Bearish remains in app.py
Buy-filter and sound-alert conditionals reference only new tier names
App launches without errors with a real depot scan
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Colour conventions: Buy=#198754/white, Bullish=light green/dark, Recovered=teal (#20c997 or similar)/dark, Neutral=grey-yellow/dark, Warning=orange (#fd7e14 or similar)/dark, Chronic=dark grey (#6c757d or similar)/white, Sell-Panic=#dc3545/white
Keep the existing _signal_colour() function pattern — just extend the dict
pitfalls: A colour mapping dict silently returns empty string for missing keys — a stock labelled Warning with no colour entry renders with no background, not an error. Verify all 7 keys are present.
Check for Bearish references in f-strings and multi-line expressions, not only in the colour dict.

key_files.note: SIGNAL_ORDER, _SIGNAL_COLOURS, Buy filters, sound alerts — all tier-label call sites

verification_steps.expected_result: No matches — zero occurrences of Bearish

verification_steps.step_text: Confirm _SIGNAL_COLOURS dict in app.py has exactly 7 keys matching the new tier names
verification_steps.expected_result: All 7 tier names present as colour mapping keys

verification_steps.step_text: Confirm SIGNAL_ORDER equals [Buy, Bullish, Recovered, Neutral, Warning, Chronic, Sell-Panic]
Launch Streamlit app and scan depot — confirm all 7 tier labels render with correct background colours
verification_steps.expected_result: No tier label shows with default/missing background

manual_tests: Warning stock shows orange background in UI
Recovered stock shows teal background in UI
Chronic stock shows dark grey background with white text in UI
Stock with RS 70-84 and 8 conditions appears as Bullish not Buy
edge_cases: Stock classified as Chronic (0-1 cond, RS>=10) renders correctly with white text on dark background

tasks.title: Update tests to cover all 7 tiers and the RS=85 Buy threshold
tasks.description: Update tests/test_classifier.py: (1) Replace all assertions referencing Bearish with Warning or Chronic as appropriate, (2) update TestSignalOrder to expect 7 entries, (3) update TestBuyTier assertions for the new RS=85 threshold (cond=8 RS=70 now returns Bullish not Buy), (4) add new test classes TestRecoveredTier, TestWarningTier, TestChronicTier covering all new paths and edge cases from the Sketch section. All existing tests must remain green — update assertions, do not delete tests.
tasks.why: The Constraints section requires all existing tests to remain green. Without updating tier-name strings in existing assertions, tests for Bearish will fail against the new Warning/Chronic outputs. Without new tests for Recovered/Warning/Chronic, the 7-tier logic has no automated coverage.
tasks.what: Edit tests/test_classifier.py: update SIGNAL_ORDER assertion to 7 entries, update Buy tier tests to use RS=85 threshold, update former Bearish tests to reference Warning, add TestRecoveredTier, TestWarningTier, TestChronicTier classes with boundary tests and worked edge cases.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
SIGNAL_ORDER test expects [Buy, Bullish, Recovered, Neutral, Warning, Chronic, Sell-Panic]
TestBuyTier updated: cond=8 RS=85 Buy, cond=8 RS=84 Bullish, cond=8 RS=70 Bullish
Former Bearish test cases updated to Warning or Chronic
New TestRecoveredTier: cond=4 RS=50 Recovered, cond=5 RS=50 Recovered, cond=4 RS=49 Neutral
New TestWarningTier: cond=3 RS=10 Warning, cond=2 RS=10 Warning, cond=3 RS=50 Warning
New TestChronicTier: cond=1 RS=10 Chronic, cond=0 RS=10 Chronic
Edge cases: cond=6 RS=40 Neutral, cond=7 RS=49 Neutral, cond=2 RS=9 Sell-Panic
All tests pass: python -m pytest tests/ -v shows zero failures
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror existing class structure (TestBuyTier, TestBullishTier, etc.) for new tier classes
Use the existing _row(n_true, rs) helper to build test rows
Do not delete existing test methods — update their expected values
pitfalls: TestBuyTier has tests for cond=8 RS=70 and RS=69 — both now return Bullish, not Buy. Update both assertions.
If test_classifier.py imports SIGNAL_ORDER, the length assertion changes from 5 to 7 — update test_length() and test_exact_content_and_order() both.

key_files.note: Update all tier-name assertions and add new test classes for Recovered, Warning, Chronic

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass, zero failures, zero errors
Test names referencing all three new tiers appear in output

unit_tests: All 7 tier return paths have at least one test each
RS boundary: RS=9 Sell-Panic, RS=10 not Sell-Panic
Buy boundary: RS=85 Buy, RS=84 Bullish
Recovered boundary: cond=4 RS=50 Recovered, cond=4 RS=49 Neutral
Bullish vs Neutral edge case: cond=6 RS=40 Neutral
edge_cases: cond=7 RS=49 Neutral (high cond count but weak RS)
cond=2 RS=9 Sell-Panic (RS override fires before Warning check)
testing_strategy.coverage_target: All 7 return paths covered by at least one explicit test

tasks.title: Smoke-test 7-tier output on live dataset and verify Success Metrics
tasks.description: Run the updated screener against the depot and manually verify the two leading-indicator Success Metrics: (1) at least one stock appears in Warning that would have shown Neutral or Bearish under the old 5-tier system on the same scan data, and (2) at least one stock that was Buy under the old system (RS 70-84, 8 conditions) now appears as Bullish. If no natural Warning candidates exist in the depot, construct a synthetic test row (cond=3, RS=30) to verify the label directly.
tasks.why: Unit tests verify the classifier logic, but only a live scan confirms the integration with screener.py, the Streamlit UI colour rendering, and the absence of runtime errors across the full pipeline.
tasks.what: Launch the Streamlit app with the existing depot CSV. Identify one Warning stock and one reclassified Bullish stock. Verify both old and new tier labels for the same row. Confirm all 7 tier colours render without missing backgrounds or Python exceptions.
tasks.where_context: app.py (Streamlit launch), depot/ (CSV input), screener.py (run pipeline)
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
At least one stock in scan output shows tier Warning with orange background
For that Warning stock: cond is 2 or 3, RS >= 10, and old 5-tier logic (cond>=2) would have returned Bearish
At least one stock shows Bullish with RS 70-84 and cond=8, confirming RS=85 Buy floor is active
Streamlit app renders all 7 tier colours without errors or missing backgrounds
No Python exceptions in the terminal during the scan
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Use existing depot CSV — no new data sources beyond yfinance already used per scan
Simulate old 5-tier check inline: cond==8 and RS>=70 was Buy, cond>=2 was Bearish
pitfalls: If the depot has no stocks with 2-3 SEPA conditions, Warning will not appear naturally — construct a synthetic row and call classify_signal() directly to verify the label.
The old Buy threshold was RS>=70 — when spot-checking reclassified stocks, compare against RS in range 70-84 with cond=8.

key_files.note: Launch point for Streamlit smoke test
Existing depot CSV data for live scan

verification_steps.step_text: Launch Streamlit app and scan the depot — confirm at least one Warning tier row with orange background
verification_steps.expected_result: At least one Warning stock visible

verification_steps.step_text: For the Warning stock: confirm cond is 2 or 3, RS >= 10, and old Bearish threshold (cond>=2) would have returned Bearish
verification_steps.expected_result: Old system returned Bearish or Neutral, new system returns Warning — 1-2 tier earlier signal confirmed

verification_steps.step_text: Find a stock with cond=8 and RS in range 70-84 — confirm it shows Bullish not Buy
verification_steps.expected_result: Bullish tier shown, RS=85 Buy floor confirmed active

verification_steps.step_text: Check terminal for Python exceptions during the scan

manual_tests: Full depot scan with all 7 tiers rendering correctly
Spot-check Warning tier against old 5-tier logic
Spot-check Bullish reclassification for RS 70-84 stocks with 8 conditions
edge_cases: If no Warning candidates in depot, inject synthetic row (cond=3, RS=30) to verify label
